questions.text: How do you typically feel when someone starts showing serious interest in you?

options.label: Excited and ready to explore where it goes
Cautiously optimistic but a bit nervous
Anxious about getting hurt or disappointed
Suddenly less interested or feeling trapped

questions.text: When do you prefer to define the relationship?

options.label: Early on - I need clarity about where things stand
After a few months when we know each other better
I wait for them to bring it up first
I prefer to keep things undefined and organic

questions.text: How do you respond when someone you're dating becomes emotionally available?

options.label: I feel more connected and want to deepen things
I appreciate it but take time to reciprocate
I feel pressure to match their emotional intensity
I start to pull back or question the relationship

questions.text: What usually happens to your past undefined relationships?

options.label: They eventually become defined or end clearly
They naturally evolve or fade out peacefully
They drag on longer than they should
They end abruptly or with confusion

questions.text: How comfortable are you with relationship labels?

options.label: Very comfortable - labels provide helpful clarity
Somewhat comfortable - depends on the situation
Uncomfortable - they feel restrictive
Very uncomfortable - I actively avoid them

questions.text: What's your pattern with emotionally unavailable people?

options.label: I recognize it early and move on
I give them time but have clear boundaries
I try to help them open up
I'm often attracted to unavailable people

questions.text: How do you feel about introducing someone you're dating to friends/family?

options.label: Excited - it's a natural step forward
Open to it when timing feels right
Hesitant - it feels like a big commitment
Resistant - I keep dating life very separate

questions.text: What happens when you start developing deeper feelings?

options.label: I communicate them openly
I show them through actions gradually
I hide them until I know they feel the same
I distance myself or sabotage the connection

questions.text: How do you handle someone asking 'What are we?'

options.label: I'm grateful they brought it up and answer honestly
I engage in the conversation thoughtfully
I deflect or say I need more time
I feel panicked or trapped by the question

questions.text: What's your relationship with being 'exclusive' but not 'official'?

options.label: I see them as basically the same thing
Exclusive is a natural step toward official
I prefer the exclusive-but-not-official phase
I resist both equally

questions.text: How do you typically end situationships?

options.label: Direct conversation about incompatibility
Gradual honest communication about needs
Slow fade - less contact over time
Ghosting or sudden disappearance

questions.text: What attracts you most in early dating?

options.label: Emotional availability and clear communication
Balanced interest with mutual effort
Mystery and chase - not knowing where I stand
Physical chemistry above all else

questions.text: How do you feel about future planning with someone you're dating?

options.label: I naturally include them in future thoughts
I'm open to making plans a few weeks out
I prefer staying present and spontaneous
Future talk makes me want to run

questions.text: What's your experience with commitment anxiety?

options.label: I rarely experience it
I feel it sometimes but work through it
It's a consistent pattern for me
It controls most of my relationship decisions

questions.text: How do you handle jealousy in undefined relationships?

options.label: I communicate my feelings and seek clarity
I reflect on whether I want exclusivity
I suffer silently since we're not official
I date other people to avoid feeling jealous

questions.text: How clearly do you express your relationship expectations?

options.label: Very clearly from early on
I communicate them as they become relevant
I hint at them but avoid direct conversation
I keep expectations vague to avoid conflict

questions.text: When you feel confused about where things stand, you:

options.label: Ask directly for clarification
Wait for the right moment to bring it up
Look for hints and clues instead of asking
Assume the worst and protect yourself emotionally

questions.text: How do you respond to mixed signals?

options.label: I address them directly and seek clarity
I observe patterns before deciding to talk
I overthink them and feel anxious
I mirror the mixed signals back

questions.text: How comfortable are you saying 'I need more from this relationship'?

options.label: Very comfortable - my needs matter
Somewhat comfortable with the right person
Uncomfortable - worried about their reaction
Very uncomfortable - I'd rather just leave

questions.text: What's your texting pattern in situationships?

options.label: Consistent and genuine communication
Responsive but not always initiating
Strategic - careful about frequency and timing
Hot and cold - varies by mood/interest level

questions.text: How do you set boundaries in undefined relationships?

options.label: Clearly and directly from the start
I communicate them as situations arise
I struggle to set boundaries without labels
I avoid setting boundaries to keep things casual

questions.text: How do you handle disagreements in situationships?

options.label: Address them directly like any relationship
Discuss them if they're important
Minimize them since we're 'not official'
Avoid conflict entirely or end things

questions.text: What's your approach to emotional vulnerability in early dating?

options.label: I'm open and authentic from the beginning
I gradually share as trust builds
I hold back until they're vulnerable first
I keep things surface-level to protect myself

questions.text: How do you communicate your availability and interest level?

options.label: Directly through words and consistent actions
Through actions more than words
Subtly - letting them figure it out
Ambiguously - keeping options open

questions.text: What happens when someone asks about your past relationships?

options.label: I share openly and honestly
I share highlights and key lessons
I give vague answers
I deflect or change the subject

questions.text: How do you handle 'the talk' about exclusivity?

options.label: I initiate it when I'm ready
I participate openly if they bring it up
I'm hesitant and need time to decide
I avoid it or end things rather than have it

questions.text: How do you handle someone who's breadcrumbing you?

options.label: I call it out and set clear expectations
I match their energy and see if it improves
I keep hoping they'll change
I'm often the one breadcrumbing

questions.text: How aware are you of your relationship patterns?

options.label: Very aware - I actively reflect on them
Somewhat aware - I notice some patterns
Limited awareness - just starting to notice
Not very aware - patterns surprise me

questions.text: What role does fear play in your relationship choices?

options.label: Minimal - I make choices from desire, not fear
Some - I notice it but work through it
Significant - fear often influences my choices
Major - fear drives most relationship decisions

questions.text: How do you typically feel after a situationship ends?

options.label: Clear about what I learned and ready to move forward
Disappointed but understanding it wasn't right
Confused about what went wrong
Stuck ruminating for a long time

questions.text: What draws you to undefined relationships?

options.label: Nothing - they're not my preference
Sometimes they just happen naturally
The freedom and lack of pressure
Fear of commitment or being fully known

questions.text: How do you handle being alone between relationships?

options.label: I enjoy it and use it for growth
I'm comfortable but miss partnership
I'm uncomfortable and quickly seek connection
I immediately look for the next person

questions.text: What patterns do you notice in who you're attracted to?

options.label: Emotionally available people who communicate well
Varied - no clear pattern
People who are inconsistent or hard to read
Emotionally unavailable or avoidant people

questions.text: How do you define a successful relationship?

options.label: Deep emotional connection and commitment
Mutual respect and healthy communication
Having fun and chemistry without drama
I'm not sure what success looks like

questions.text: How much do your past experiences influence current dating?

options.label: I've processed them and moved forward
Some influence but I'm aware of it
Significant influence - still healing
Heavy influence - patterns keep repeating

questions.text: What's your biggest fear in relationships?

options.label: Not finding the right person
Being hurt or disappointed
Losing myself or my independence
Being trapped or controlled

questions.text: How do you react when someone mirrors your unavailability?

options.label: I don't tend to be unavailable
I appreciate the space but stay connected
It bothers me - I want them to pursue
I suddenly become more interested

questions.text: What role does self-esteem play in your relationship choices?

options.label: I have healthy self-esteem that guides good choices
Generally positive but occasionally doubt myself
Low self-esteem affects my choices often
I accept less than I deserve regularly

questions.text: How do you feel about someone who's 'too available'?

options.label: I appreciate their interest and consistency
It's nice but I need them to have their own life
It makes me less interested
It's a major turn-off

questions.text: How emotionally available are you right now?

options.label: Very available - ready for real connection
Mostly available - working through minor blocks
Somewhat unavailable - still processing past hurt
Emotionally unavailable - not ready for serious connection

questions.text: What happens when things start getting 'real'?

options.label: I lean in and embrace deeper connection
I proceed cautiously but stay present
I feel scared and need space
I find reasons why it won't work

questions.text: How do you show care in early dating?

options.label: Openly through words and thoughtful actions
Primarily through actions and presence
Subtly - afraid of seeming too interested
I hold back to protect myself

questions.text: How comfortable are you receiving emotional support?

options.label: Very comfortable - I can be vulnerable
Somewhat comfortable with the right person
Uncomfortable - I prefer being self-sufficient
Very uncomfortable - I avoid it

questions.text: What's your relationship with emotional intimacy?

options.label: I crave and pursue deep emotional connection
I value it but need time to build it
It makes me uncomfortable or vulnerable
I actively avoid it

questions.text: How do you handle someone being emotionally needy?

options.label: With patience and clear communication about balance
I try to meet their needs while maintaining boundaries
I feel overwhelmed and withdraw
I immediately distance myself

questions.text: How do you process feelings about someone you're dating?

options.label: I acknowledge them and communicate openly
I reflect privately then share when ready
I suppress them to avoid vulnerability
I distract myself or deny them

questions.text: What's your capacity for sustained emotional presence?

options.label: High - I can maintain connection consistently
Good - with occasional need for space
Limited - I need frequent breaks
Low - I struggle with sustained connection

questions.text: How do you respond to someone crying or being very emotional?

options.label: I move toward them and provide comfort
I stay present and ask how I can help
I feel awkward but try to be supportive
I feel uncomfortable and don't know what to do

questions.text: What's your pattern with vulnerability?

options.label: I share openly and invite reciprocal vulnerability
I'm vulnerable when it feels safe
I'm vulnerable only after they are
I avoid vulnerability at all costs

questions.text: How ready are you for a committed relationship right now?

options.label: Very ready - actively seeking commitment
Open to it with the right person
Uncertain - says yes but acts unsure
Not ready - prefer keeping things casual

archetypes.name: The Conscious Dater
archetypes.description: You have strong self-awareness and clear boundaries. You're emotionally available and communicate your needs effectively. Situationships aren't your pattern - you naturally move toward defined relationships or move on.
suggestions: Continue trusting your instincts about what you need
Help potential partners understand your communication style
Be patient with those who need more time to open up
Remember that your clarity is a strength, not a flaw

archetypes.name: The Anxious Attacher
archetypes.description: You crave connection but fear rejection, often staying in undefined situations hoping they'll evolve. You're highly attuned to others' emotions but may neglect your own needs. Your relationships lack clarity because you're afraid to rock the boat.
characteristics: Stays in situations hoping for change
suggestions: Practice stating your needs without apologizing
Build self-worth independent of relationship status
Notice when you're pursuing emotionally unavailable people
Set a timeline for clarity and honor it
Work on self-soothing anxiety rather than seeking reassurance

archetypes.name: The Avoidant Attacher
archetypes.description: You value independence and feel trapped by commitment. When things get serious, you pull away or find flaws. Situationships feel safer because they don't require full vulnerability or merging of lives.
suggestions: Explore what commitment means to you (it doesn't mean losing yourself)
Practice small acts of vulnerability regularly
Notice when you're self-sabotaging good connections
Work on communicating needs instead of disappearing
Consider whether past hurts are blocking present opportunities

archetypes.name: The Healer/Fixer
archetypes.description: You're drawn to potential rather than reality. You stay in undefined relationships trying to help someone become emotionally available. Your empathy is a gift, but you often give more than you receive.
characteristics: Neglects own needs while helping others
suggestions: Date people for who they are now, not who they could be
Notice the pattern of choosing emotionally unavailable people
Practice receiving as much as you give
Set boundaries around emotional labor
Redirect helping energy toward yourself and your growth

archetypes.name: The Serial Situationshipper
archetypes.description: Multiple undefined relationships is your comfort zone. You might be avoiding deeper work, staying busy with surface connections. You're excellent at starting things but struggle with deepening them.
suggestions: Explore what you're avoiding by keeping things surface
Try being single for a period to understand yourself
Practice staying through discomfort instead of moving on
Build emotional capacity through therapy or self-work
Date with intention rather than distraction

archetypes.name: The Fearful Attacher
archetypes.description: You want connection but fear getting hurt. You send mixed signals because you're genuinely conflicted. You pursue then withdraw, creating the chaos you're trying to avoid. Situationships match your internal ambivalence.
suggestions: Work on healing past relationship trauma
Practice consistent behavior even when scared
Communicate your fears instead of acting them out
Build secure friendships as a foundation
Consider therapy to process attachment wounds
Be honest when you're not ready for relationship
